# DCC format fixtures

Format tests generate small workbooks at runtime when the corresponding writer
is deterministic and installed. Legacy XLS coverage uses the readxl package's
installed XLS fixture because DCC does not add a legacy XLS writer dependency.

Every fixture must declare its worksheet and range. A backend opening a file is
not sufficient for Stable status; semantic parity and the Phase E platform
matrix remain required.

`manifest.json` is generated by `tools/generate-format-fixtures.R`. It records
source and file SHA-256 hashes, encoding, compression, expected canonical hash,
labels, missing-state count, and the current capability level. Small synthetic
fixtures live under `generated/`; no real survey data are used.

Committed fixtures are immutable by default because container and statistical
writers may embed timestamps or generator metadata. A normal generator run
refreshes the manifest without overwriting evidence files. Maintainers must use
`Rscript tools/generate-format-fixtures.R --force` to deliberately rebuild the
fixture set, then review and commit every changed hash.

Formats without a deterministic writer in DCC's installed dependencies are
recorded as `external_required`. That is a known evidence gap, not a pass, and
the verifier rejects any attempt to call such a format Stable. Cross-platform
Stable status requires matching Linux, macOS, and Windows evidence.
